message.content:
- **Day 1–2: Map your people & tools.**  
  - Make a one-page doc: manager name, core teammates, what each does, and how to contact them (Slack channel, email).  
  - List every tool you’ll use (e.g., Slack, Jira, Notion) and do one tiny action in each (send 1 intro message, open 1 ticket, comment once).

- **Daily: 2 short check-ins with yourself.**  
  - Morning: write 3 tasks on a sticky note or in a note app, each 20–30 minutes max (e.g., “Read onboarding doc pages 1–5,” “Set up calendar,” “Shadow 1 meeting and take 3 notes”).  
  - End of day: mark what you finished, move anything left to tomorrow, and write 1 question to ask your manager next day.

- **First meeting with your manager: get 3 specifics.**  
  - Ask: “What does a successful first week look like in 1–2 sentences?” and write it down word-for-word.  
  - Ask for 1 small real task you can do this week (e.g., update a doc, test a feature, draft an email), not just reading.  
  - Ask which Slack channels, folders, and recurring meetings you must join today—do it during or immediately after the call.

- **End of week: send a 5–6 line recap message.**  
  - In Slack/email: 3 bullet points of what you did, 1–2 things you’re stuck on, 1 concrete plan for next week (e.g., “Finish X doc,” “Shadow Y process”).  
  - Use that message as your agenda for your next 1:1 so you don’t have to remember everything on the spot.
